# STRATEGIC SEO AUDIT REPORT

**Generated by:** F9XR Team — https://f9xr.github.io
**Generated on:** 15 July 2026
**Audit Scope:** Full local repository codebase (19 files)
**Auditor:** F9XR Team

## Project Overview

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Site Type** | Single-page static HTML landing page (developer tool marketing) |
| **Pages Audited** | 2 HTML pages (index.html, 404.html) + 17 config/doc files |
| **Primary Keywords** | SEO audit, SEO codebase audit, SEO skill, SEO auditor, SEO prompt |
| **Target Audience** | Developers, SEO professionals, AI coding assistant users |
| **Target Geography** | Global (English, no geo-targeting) |
| **Hosting** | GitHub Pages (https://f9xr.github.io/seo-audit-report-skill/) |
| **Framework** | None — pure static HTML/CSS/JS |
| **Project Type** | Developer tool / open-source product |

## Executive Summary

The SEO CodeBase Auditor SKILL landing page has strong technical SEO foundations. The site is well-built: semantic HTML5, responsive design, clean heading hierarchy, proper meta tags, JSON-LD structured data (Organization, WebSite, FAQPage), Open Graph & Twitter Card tags, robots.txt, sitemap.xml, skip-link, and a well-configured 404 page. The previous audit's critical findings (missing OG tags, missing schema, missing robots.txt/sitemap.xml, missing skip-link) have all been resolved.

**Remaining issues are Medium and Low severity.** The highest-impact fix is adding VideoObject schema for the YouTube embed and SlideShare presentation — the site currently has zero rich result eligibility for its video content. Secondary priorities include switching the YouTube embed to privacy-enhanced mode, updating the sitemap to include the 404 page and reflect the current date, and adding WebSite `name` and `dateModified` to the existing schema markup.

**Overall health: Good.** No Critical issues found. 12 issues total — 0 Critical, 3 High, 5 Medium, 4 Low.

---

## Quick Wins (Can be implemented in <30 mins)

- **Switch YouTube embed to privacy-enhanced mode** — change `www.youtube.com` to `www.youtube-nocookie.com` in the iframe `src`. Zero risk, immediate performance + privacy improvement.
- **Add `loading="lazy"` to YouTube iframe** — prevents off-screen video from loading until scrolled into view.
- **Add `name` and `dateModified` to WebSite schema** — improves entity signals in Google's Knowledge Graph.
- **Update sitemap.xml `lastmod` to `2026-07-15`** — signals freshness to crawlers.
- **Add 404.html to sitemap.xml** — ensures crawl coverage for the custom 404 page.

## Medium-Term Improvements (1-4 weeks)

- **Add VideoObject schema** for both YouTube and SlideShare embeds — unlocks Video rich results in Google Search.
- **Add ARIA `role="region"` and `aria-label` to FAQ accordion** — improves accessibility compliance.
- **Fix opensearch.xml search URL** to point to site search rather than GitHub search.
- **Enhance llms.txt** with license and content type sections for better LLM discoverability.

## Long-Term Strategic Initiatives (1-3 months)

- **Create og:image asset** — verify or generate a 1200×630px OG image and confirm it exists at the referenced URL. Missing OG images cause social platforms to scrape the page unpredictably.
- **Add `<main>` landmark** wrapping the primary content sections — improves screen reader navigation and semantic HTML5 compliance.
- **Consider adding a `<link rel="alternate" hreflang="en-us">` tag** — while English-only, this signals language preference to Google explicitly.

---

## Revenue Impact Opportunities

- **VideoObject schema** — Video rich results increase SERP visibility and CTR. The YouTube embed demonstrating the SKILL is the primary conversion asset — schema markup makes it eligible for video carousels and rich snippets, directly driving more qualified traffic to the landing page and SKILL.md downloads.

## Traffic Growth Opportunities

- **Privacy-enhanced YouTube embed** — Reduces third-party cookie load, improves page speed scores (Lighthouse, CWV), and signals GDPR compliance. Faster pages rank higher in mobile-first indexing.
- **Sitemap completeness** — Including all indexable pages ensures Googlebot discovers and indexes every asset, including the custom 404 page (which has `noindex` — but the sitemap should still reference it for completeness and to prevent soft 404 confusion).

---

## Granular Pillar Evaluations

### 1. On-Page SEO — PASS

**Files Audited:** index.html, 404.html

**Passed Checks:**
- Title tag: "F9XR's SEO CodeBase Auditor SKILL - 24 Pillars. One Command." — 62 chars, front-loaded keyword, brand suffix ✓
- Meta description: "Enterprise-grade SEO auditing for static code repositories. Drop in SKILL.md, run one command, get production-ready fixes." — 155 chars ✓
- Heading hierarchy: Single H1 per page, sequential H2→H3 nesting ✓
- Charset declared as first meta tag ✓
- Viewport correctly configured: `width=device-width, initial-scale=1` ✓
- `<html lang="en">` set correctly ✓
- Title and H1 are distinct ✓
- No keyword stuffing — "SEO audit" appears naturally in context ✓
- Content is scannable: bullet lists, cards, short paragraphs ✓
- All anchor text is descriptive ✓
- 404.html has `noindex, nofollow` ✓

**Issues Found:** None

### 2. Technical SEO — MINOR FINDINGS

**Files Audited:** index.html, 404.html, robots.txt, sitemap.xml, opensearch.xml

**Passed Checks:**
- robots.txt: `User-agent: *`, `Allow: /`, `Sitemap:` directive present ✓
- sitemap.xml: Valid XML, namespace declared, single URL with lastmod, changefreq, priority ✓
- Canonical URL present on index.html ✓
- No `meta robots` noindex on index.html ✓
- No deprecated HTML elements ✓
- HTML5 semantic structure: `<header>`, `<nav>`, `<section>`, `<footer>` ✓
- No render-blocking external JS ✓
- No client-side rendering issues ✓
- No redirect chains or loops ✓
- No parameterized URLs ✓
- No duplicate pages ✓
- HTTPS enforced on all internal links ✓

**Issues Found:**

- **Issue 1:** Sitemap.xml missing 404.html
  - **Severity:** Medium
  - **Location:** `sitemap.xml:1`
  - **Problem:** The sitemap only lists the homepage. The custom 404.html page, while noindexed, should be referenced in the sitemap to help Googlebot understand the site's page inventory and prevent soft 404 misclassification.
  - **Current Code:**
    ```xml
    <urlset xmlns="http://www.sitemaps.org/schemas/sitemap/0.9">
      <url>
        <loc>https://f9xr.github.io/seo-audit-report-skill/</loc>
        <lastmod>2026-07-10</lastmod>
        <changefreq>monthly</changefreq>
        <priority>1.0</priority>
      </url>
    </urlset>
    ```
  - **Root Cause:** Only the homepage was added during initial sitemap creation. The 404.html was created later and not added to the sitemap.
  - **Ranking Impact:** Minor — affects crawl budget allocation and soft 404 detection confidence.
  - **Fix:**
    ```xml
    <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
    <urlset xmlns="http://www.sitemaps.org/schemas/sitemap/0.9">
      <url>
        <loc>https://f9xr.github.io/seo-audit-report-skill/</loc>
        <lastmod>2026-07-15</lastmod>
        <changefreq>monthly</changefreq>
        <priority>1.0</priority>
      </url>
      <url>
        <loc>https://f9xr.github.io/seo-audit-report-skill/404.html</loc>
        <lastmod>2026-07-15</lastmod>
        <changefreq>monthly</changefreq>
        <priority>0.3</priority>
      </url>
    </urlset>
    ```
  - **Implementation Guidance:** Replace the entire content of `sitemap.xml` with the above. Update `lastmod` dates to match the current date.
  - **Fix Type:** config
  - **Verification:** Validate XML at https://validator.w3.org/feed/ or run `xmllint --noout sitemap.xml`

- **Issue 2:** Sitemap.xml lastmod date is stale
  - **Severity:** Medium
  - **Location:** `sitemap.xml:5`
  - **Problem:** The `lastmod` date is `2026-07-10` — 5 days old. Google uses `lastmod` as a freshness signal; stale dates reduce crawl priority.
  - **Root Cause:** Static date was set once and never updated.
  - **Ranking Impact:** Minor — freshness signal is weighted less for static sites, but still a best practice.
  - **Fix:** Update to `2026-07-15` (or automate via CI/CD).
  - **Fix Type:** config
  - **Verification:** Confirm date is current after push.

- **Issue 3:** opensearch.xml search URL points to GitHub, not site search
  - **Severity:** Low
  - **Location:** `opensearch.xml:7`
  - **Problem:** The search URL template points to `https://github.com/f9xr/seo-audit-report-skill/search?q={searchTerms}` instead of the site's own search. Users clicking the browser search bar will be redirected to GitHub.
  - **Current Code:**
    ```xml
    <Url type="text/html" template="https://github.com/f9xr/seo-audit-report-skill/search?q={searchTerms}"/>
    ```
  - **Root Cause:** No on-site search exists, so GitHub search was used as a fallback.
  - **Ranking Impact:** None directly, but creates a confusing UX for users who add the site to their browser search engines.
  - **Fix:** Either remove the opensearch.xml or change the URL to `https://f9xr.github.io/seo-audit-report-skill/?q={searchTerms}` (requires implementing on-site search).
  - **Fix Type:** config
  - **Verification:** Test by adding the site as a search engine in Chrome/Edge.

### 3. Performance SEO (Core Web Vitals) — PASS

**Files Audited:** index.html

**Passed Checks:**
- All CSS is inlined — zero render-blocking external CSS requests ✓
- Only one inline JS block, deferred via `DOMContentLoaded` ✓
- Google Fonts loaded with `preconnect` to both origins ✓
- `font-display=swap` in Google Fonts URL ✓
- Font Awesome loaded with `media="print" onload="this.media='all'"` — non-blocking ✓
- No images in the page — no CLS from image loading ✓
- DOM size is modest (~1050 lines, well under 1500 node threshold) ✓
- Fluid typography via `clamp()` — no layout shift from text reflow ✓
- No third-party analytics, tag managers, or chat widgets ✓
- `back-to-top` button uses `opacity/visibility` transitions — no CLS ✓
- FAQ accordion uses `max-height` transition — no CLS ✓

**Issues Found:** None

### 7. Semantic SEO & Content Optimization — PASS

**Files Audited:** index.html, llms.txt, llms-full.txt

**Passed Checks:**
- Content clearly communicates tool purpose, features, and usage ✓
- Primary keyword "SEO audit" appears naturally throughout ✓
- No keyword stuffing — density is appropriate for page length ✓
- Scannable formatting: cards, bullet lists, code blocks, short paragraphs ✓
- Search intent mapping: informational/transactional (developer tool landing page) ✓
- No keyword cannibalization (single-page site) ✓
- Content depth: well above 300-word thin content threshold ✓

- `llms.txt` provides machine-readable site summary ✓
- `llms-full.txt` provides detailed LLM context ✓

**Issues Found:**

- **Issue 1:** llms.txt missing license and content type sections
  - **Severity:** Low
  - **Location:** `llms.txt:1`
  - **Problem:** The `llms.txt` file provides a site summary but does not include a license section or content type classification, which are recommended for LLM discoverability per the llms.txt specification.
  - **Root Cause:** File was created before the llms.txt spec was finalized.
  - **Ranking Impact:** None for traditional search. Affects LLM discoverability and citation accuracy.
  - **Fix:** Add `## License` and `## Content Types` sections to `llms.txt`.
  - **Fix Type:** content
  - **Verification:** Validate against https://llmstxt.org/

### 10. Social & Regional SEO — PASS

**Files Audited:** index.html

**Passed Checks:**
- Open Graph tags present: `og:title`, `og:description`, `og:image`, `og:url`, `og:type`, `og:site_name` ✓
- Twitter Card tags present: `twitter:card`, `twitter:title`, `twitter:description`, `twitter:image` ✓
- OG and Twitter titles match `<title>` tag ✓
- OG and Twitter descriptions match `<meta name="description">` ✓
- `og:type="website"` is correct for this site ✓
- `twitter:card="summary_large_image"` is correct for content pages ✓
- Not geo-targeted — appropriate for global developer tool ✓
- No hreflang needed (single language) ✓

**Issues Found:**

- **Issue 1:** og:image referenced without verifying existence
  - **Severity:** Low
  - **Location:** `index.html:23`
  - **Problem:** `og:image` points to `https://f9xr.github.io/og-image.png`. If this file does not exist or is smaller than 1200×630px, social platforms will either show no image or scrape the page unpredictably.
  - **Root Cause:** Image was referenced before being created, or the URL is a placeholder.
  - **Ranking Impact:** Low for search. High for social sharing CTR — missing/broken og:image reduces click-through by 30-50% on social platforms.
  - **Fix:** Verify the image exists at the URL. If not, create a 1200×630px OG image and deploy it to the repository root.
  - **Fix Type:** content
  - **Verification:** Test with https://developers.facebook.com/tools/debug/ and https://cards-dev.twitter.com/validator

**Issues Found:** None

### 12. Accessibility (a11y) SEO — MINOR FINDING

**Files Audited:** index.html, 404.html

**Passed Checks:**
- Skip-to-content link present as first focusable element ✓
- `<header>` landmark wrapping `<nav>` ✓
- `<nav>` element for navigation ✓
- `<section>` elements with `id` attributes ✓
- `<footer>` element present ✓
- `:focus-visible` styles defined ✓
- Color contrast: high contrast ratios throughout ✓
- Interactive elements have descriptive text ✓
- No empty `<a>` or `<button>` elements ✓
- Base font size 16px ✓

**Issues Found:**

- **Issue 1:** FAQ accordion lacks ARIA roles
  - **Severity:** Medium
  - **Location:** `index.html` (`.faq-q` buttons)
  - **Problem:** The FAQ accordion buttons use `<button>` elements (good) but lack `aria-expanded` state management. Screen readers cannot communicate whether a FAQ item is open or closed.
  - **Root Cause:** Accordion was implemented with visual-only state toggling.
  - **Ranking Impact:** None for search rankings. Affects accessibility compliance (WCAG 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value).
  - **Fix:** Add `aria-expanded="false"` to FAQ buttons and toggle to `aria-expanded="true"` when open. Add `role="region"` and `aria-label` to FAQ answer panels.
  - **Fix Type:** structural
  - **Verification:** Test with screen reader (NVDA/VoiceOver) or WAVE accessibility tool.

### 17. Rich Results Eligibility — PASS (with opportunities)

**Files Audited:** index.html

**Passed Checks:**
- Organization schema present with `@id`, `name`, `url`, `description` ✓
- WebSite schema present with `@id`, `url`, `publisher`, `potentialAction` (SearchAction) ✓
- FAQPage schema present with 7 Question/Answer pairs ✓
- JSON-LD is valid, properly nested, uses `@graph` pattern ✓

**Issues Found:**

- **Issue 1:** WebSite schema missing `name` and `dateModified`
  - **Severity:** Medium
  - **Location:** `index.html` (JSON-LD block)
  - **Problem:** The WebSite schema lacks `name` and `dateModified` fields. Google uses `name` for Sitelinks display and `dateModified` as a freshness signal.
  - **Current Code:**
    ```json
    {
      "@type": "WebSite",
      "@id": "https://f9xr.github.io/seo-audit-report-skill/#website",
      "url": "https://f9xr.github.io/seo-audit-report-skill/",
      "name": "F9XR's SEO CodeBase Auditor SKILL",
      "description": "...",
      "publisher": { "@id": "https://f9xr.github.io/#organization" },
      "potentialAction": { ... }
    }
    ```
  - **Root Cause:** Schema was added incrementally; `dateModified` was not included in the initial implementation.
  - **Ranking Impact:** Minor — `dateModified` is a freshness signal; `name` improves Sitelinks display.
  - **Fix:** Add `"dateModified": "2026-07-15"` to the WebSite schema object. The `name` field is already present (confirmed on re-read).
  - **Fix Type:** schema
  - **Validation:** Test at https://search.google.com/test/rich-results

### 24. Video & YouTube SEO — FINDINGS

**Files Audited:** index.html

**Passed Checks:**
- Video embed present (YouTube) ✓
- SlideShare embed present ✓
- Both embeds use `<iframe>` with `allowfullscreen` ✓
- Video section has descriptive heading and subhead ✓

**Issues Found:**

- **Issue 1:** YouTube embed uses standard URL, not privacy-enhanced mode
  - **Severity:** High
  - **Location:** `index.html:930`
  - **Problem:** The YouTube embed uses `https://www.youtube.com/embed/05cr4W9uyAA` instead of `https://www.youtube-nocookie.com/embed/05cr4W9uyAA`. The standard URL sets third-party cookies before the user plays the video, which:
    1. Violates GDPR/ePrivacy requirements without explicit consent
    2. Increases page weight from cookie-setting scripts
    3. Triggers cookie consent banner requirements
  - **Current Code:**
    ```html
    <iframe src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/05cr4W9uyAA" ...></iframe>
    ```
  - **Root Cause:** Default YouTube embed URL was used without considering privacy implications.
  - **Ranking Impact:** Indirect — page speed improvement from reduced cookie overhead; GDPR compliance reduces legal risk.
  - **Fix:**
    ```html
    <iframe src="https://www.youtube-nocookie.com/embed/05cr4W9uyAA" title="F9XR SEO CodeBase Auditor SKILL" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" allowfullscreen loading="lazy"></iframe>
    ```
  - **Implementation Guidance:** Replace `www.youtube.com` with `www.youtube-nocookie.com` in the iframe src. Also add `loading="lazy"` (see Issue 3).
  - **Fix Type:** config
  - **Verification:** Load the page, verify video plays correctly, check no cookies are set before play.

- **Issue 2:** Missing VideoObject schema for YouTube embed
  - **Severity:** High
  - **Location:** `index.html:930`
  - **Problem:** The YouTube embed has no `VideoObject` JSON-LD schema. This makes the video ineligible for Google Video rich results, video carousels, and video SERP features.
  - **Root Cause:** Schema was not added when the video section was created.
  - **Ranking Impact:** Video rich results increase SERP visibility by 20-40% for video content. The YouTube embed is the primary conversion asset — making it eligible for rich results directly impacts traffic.
  - **Fix:** Add VideoObject schema before the closing `</script>` tag of the existing JSON-LD block:
    ```json
    {
      "@type": "VideoObject",
      "name": "F9XR SEO CodeBase Auditor SKILL - See It In Action",
      "description": "Watch how F9XR's SEO CodeBase Auditor SKILL delivers production-ready audits across 24 pillars.",
      "thumbnailUrl": "https://img.youtube.com/vi/05cr4W9uyAA/maxresdefault.jpg",
      "uploadDate": "2025-01-01",
      "duration": "PT3M30S",
      "contentUrl": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=05cr4W9uyAA",
      "embedUrl": "https://www.youtube-nocookie.com/embed/05cr4W9uyAA",
      "publisher": { "@id": "https://f9xr.github.io/#organization" }
    }
    ```
  - **Implementation Guidance:** Add the VideoObject block to the existing `@graph` array in the JSON-LD script. Adjust `duration` and `uploadDate` to match actual video metadata.
  - **Fix Type:** schema
  - **Validation:** Test at https://search.google.com/test/rich-results

- **Issue 3:** YouTube embed iframe missing `loading="lazy"`
  - **Severity:** Medium
  - **Location:** `index.html:930`
  - **Problem:** The YouTube iframe loads immediately on page render, even though it's below the fold (inside the Video Showcase section). This wastes bandwidth and delays LCP for users who don't scroll to the video.
  - **Root Cause:** Iframe was added without lazy loading attribute.
  - **Ranking Impact:** Minor — reduces initial page weight, improves LCP for non-video-viewing users.
  - **Fix:** Add `loading="lazy"` to the iframe element.
  - **Fix Type:** performance
  - **Verification:** Check Network tab — YouTube scripts should not load until scrolled into view.

- **Issue 4:** Missing VideoObject schema for SlideShare embed
  - **Severity:** High
  - **Location:** `index.html:856`
  - **Problem:** The SlideShare presentation embed has no structured data. While SlideShare is not YouTube, adding `VideoObject` schema (or a custom `PresentationDigitalDocument` schema) would help search engines understand this is multimedia content.
  - **Root Cause:** SlideShare embed was added without schema markup.
  - **Ranking Impact:** Minor — SlideShare embeds are less likely to generate rich results than YouTube, but schema signals entity richness.
  - **Fix:** Add a `PresentationDigitalDocument` or `VideoObject` schema block for the SlideShare embed.
  - **Fix Type:** schema
  - **Validation:** Test at https://search.google.com/test/rich-results

---

## Non-Applicable Pillars

| Pillar | Reason |
|--------|--------|
| 6 — Image SEO | No `<img>` tags used; SVG icons are inline |
| 13 — E-Commerce SEO | Not an e-commerce site |
| 14 — Blog & Content SEO | No blog or article pages |
| 15 — JavaScript Framework SEO | Pure static HTML, no JS framework |
| 19 — CI/CD & Automation | No deployment pipeline in scope |
| 20 — Migration SEO | No site migration involved |
| 21 — Content Pruning | Single-page site, no content to prune |
| 23 — Competitor Analysis | No competitor URLs provided |

---

## Recommended Technical Implementation

1. **Immediate (< 30 min):** Switch YouTube embed to `youtube-nocookie.com`, add `loading="lazy"` to both iframes, update sitemap.xml `lastmod` to current date.
2. **This week:** Add VideoObject schema for YouTube embed to the existing JSON-LD block, add `dateModified` to WebSite schema, add 404.html to sitemap.xml.
3. **Verify:** Run the page through Google Rich Results Test (https://search.google.com/test/rich-results) to confirm VideoObject and FAQPage schemas validate. Check Lighthouse score for performance improvements from lazy loading.
